name: HBV_full_genome
description: Hepatitis B virus complete genome: partially double-stranded circular relaxed-circular DNA (rcDNA), ~3.2 kb, replicated via an RNA intermediate (pgRNA) using viral reverse transcriptase. The genome is functionally circular and uses four overlapping open reading frames (P/polymerase, S/surface, C/core, X) that together encode 7 proteins through alternative initiation. Reference genotype A (NC_003977.2, Galibert 1979 / Norder revision). HBV exists as 10 major genotypes (A-J) with up to 8% intergenotype divergence; intra-genotype divergence <4%. Maps as a single locus but covalently closed circular (cccDNA) intermediate in nucleus and integrated forms in chronic infection require circular-aware mapping and host-junction detection.
expected_copy_count: 10^4-10^9 rcDNA copies per infected hepatocyte in chronic HBV; integrated copies sub-clonal in hepatocellular carcinoma
